title: How wounds heal
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Wounds heal over time through staged tissue repair.
Patient education may include keeping wounds clean, protecting healing tissue, and following clinician dressing instructions.
Scars can continue to change after the wound surface closes.
Warning signs include increasing pain, redness, swelling, drainage, fever, or wound opening.
